behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (classifier -> API client payload), not model judgment. A fake client that captures outbound payload can catch missing/partial taxonomy lists. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about request contents, not real HTTP/OS effects (disqualifies System) and not output quality/non-deterministic model decisions (disqualifies Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a test double API client, call classify(), and assert captured request includes the full configured taxonomy labels exactly once before dispatch.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is local normalization/validation logic: map out-of-taxonomy label to "unknown". Real infrastructure and real model behavior are unnecessary; mocking would not hide this specific failure because the failure is in deterministic post-processing.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/validation path with raw label values not present in taxonomy (e.g., "medical") and assert final label is "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets determinism of one local normalization step for identical input. No module wiring, OS/network, or model output quality is required to expose this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and assert identical final label every run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency of model-produced labels. A stub would mask real provider/model variance, so Integration is insufficient. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision type repeated, not multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real provider calls with identical document/taxonomy under fixed params, record raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label changes only when raw provider output changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at cross-module handoff: tokenizer/counting + policy + dispatch gate. In-process wiring is sufficient; no real HTTP/OS needed. Rejected higher levels: behavior is pre-dispatch enforcement, not provider/model-output-dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small budget, run classify() with oversized document, and assert module applies configured policy (truncate or reject) and that outbound request/input sent to client conforms to budget gate.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real AI provider call and real provider token accounting; mocks can trivially pass and hide mismatches between local estimation and provider-reported usage. Rejected Integration due explicit dependence on real AI API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for a fixed canary document set, collect provider-reported token usage, and assert each call is <= configured budget (with fixed model/version and parameters).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Hard-case canary gate: include fixed high-token canaries before broader corpus runs to catch boundary regressions early.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across local modules (error classifier, backoff policy, retry loop). A controlled fake API that returns rate-limit then success can reveal this. Rejected System: real network behavior is not required to validate wait+retry decision path. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API client that returns rate-limit signal first then success; assert classifier delays per policy, retries expected times, and returns successful label instead of bubbling initial error.
